mission: Determine whether the future physical-surface selector can exist as a current-stack equivariant observable on the neutral puncture sector, or whether any nontrivial visible/compensator split must break puncture symmetry explicitly.

theorem_1_equivariant_selector_weights_a.statement: Represent a candidate visible-sector selector on an n-puncture neutral tuple by slot weights w_i in {0,1}, so q_vis(a) = sum_i w_i a_i mod k. If the selector is equivariant under the full puncture permutation action, then sigma.w = w for every sigma in Sym_n. Hence all w_i are equal. Idempotence then forces only the trivial choices w = 0 or w = (1,...,1).

formulas.visible_charge: q_vis(a) = sum_i w_i a_i mod k
formulas.equivariance: sigma.w = w   for all sigma in Sym_n
formulas.conclusion: w_i = c for all i,   c in {0,1}

theorem_2_nontrivial_equivariant_visible.statement: For the only equivariant idempotent weights w = c(1,...,1), the induced visible charge is q_vis(a) = c sum_i a_i = 0 mod k on every physical neutral tuple. Therefore no nontrivial visible/compensator split with q != 0 can be realized by a current-stack equivariant selector.

formulas.visible_charge_reduction: q_vis(a) = c sum_i a_i = 0 mod k

theorem_3_exact_future_surface_object.statement: The remaining positive surface route is not an arbitrary projector P_vis. It is a symmetry-breaking selector B_sel together with a nonzero charge q in Z_k. Any positive future theorem must therefore add a genuine symmetry-breaking surface datum or state; it cannot arise from the current tracial puncture-symmetric sector alone.

imports.previous_surface_boundary: The future compensator route is now reduced from a geometric marked defect to one exact physical-surface datum: a sector selector P_vis together with a nonzero charge q in Z_k. On the current stack that selector is absent.

final_boundary.safe_statement: No nontrivial visible-sector selector exists as a current-stack equivariant observable on the neutral puncture sector. The only surviving positive surface route is an explicitly symmetry-breaking selector B_sel with nonzero q.
